intro: Calculate CPM (cost per 1,000 impressions), CPC (cost per click), and CTR (click-through rate) for your advertising campaigns. Enter your impression count, total spend, and optional clicks to get all three metrics instantly.
howItWorks: CPM = (Total Cost ÷ Impressions) × 1,000. CPC = Total Cost ÷ Clicks (if clicks are provided). CTR = (Clicks ÷ Impressions) × 100. These three metrics together give a complete picture of campaign efficiency. Low CPM means cheap reach; high CTR means engaging creatives; low CPC means affordable traffic.
interpretationGuide: CPM benchmarks vary widely by platform and industry. Google Display averages £1–3 CPM; Facebook/Instagram £5–15; LinkedIn £25–50; YouTube £2–8. A low CPM with low CTR means impressions are cheap but not engaging. A high CTR with high CPC suggests your audience is interested but you're bidding in a competitive market. Use this alongside your break-even and ROI calculators.

faqs.question: What does CPM stand for?
faqs.answer: CPM stands for Cost Per Mille — 'mille' is Latin for thousand. It represents the cost to show your ad 1,000 times, regardless of how many people click. It is the primary pricing model for brand awareness and display advertising.

faqs.question: What is a good CPM?
faqs.answer: Good CPM depends on the platform and industry. Google Display: £1–5. Facebook/Instagram: £5–15. LinkedIn: £30–80. YouTube: £2–10. A low CPM is only good if those impressions reach your target audience — cheap, irrelevant traffic has no value.

faqs.question: What is CPC?
faqs.answer: CPC is Cost Per Click — the average amount you pay each time someone clicks your ad. Lower is better, but only if the traffic converts. A £0.10 CPC is worthless if no one buys; a £5 CPC is fine if each customer is worth £500.

faqs.question: What is a good CTR?
faqs.answer: CTR varies by ad type and platform. Search ads average 2–5% CTR; display ads average 0.1–0.5%; social media ads 0.5–2%. A CTR above average for your platform suggests your creative and targeting are well-matched to your audience.

faqs.question: How is CPM different from CPC?
faqs.answer: CPM campaigns charge per thousand impressions regardless of clicks — used for brand awareness. CPC campaigns charge only when someone clicks — used for performance marketing where you want direct traffic. The same spend buys different things depending on the model.

faqs.question: How do I use CPM to estimate campaign reach?
faqs.answer: Reach = (Budget ÷ CPM) × 1,000. With a £500 budget and £10 CPM, you'll get 50,000 impressions. If your frequency (average views per person) is 3, that's ~16,667 unique people reached.

faqs.question: What is eCPM?
faqs.answer: Effective CPM (eCPM) is used by publishers to measure revenue per 1,000 ad impressions regardless of the pricing model. eCPM = (Total Revenue ÷ Impressions) × 1,000. It normalises CPC and CPM campaigns to compare performance.

faqs.question: How do I lower my CPM?
faqs.answer: CPM is largely set by market supply and demand. You can lower it by: broadening your audience (less competitive targeting), using lower-CPM platforms, running at off-peak times, testing cheaper ad formats (e.g., static images vs. video), and improving relevance scores.

faqs.question: What is impression frequency and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Frequency is how many times the same person sees your ad. High frequency (>7 on a short campaign) causes ad fatigue and wasted spend. Most platforms let you cap frequency. Monitor it alongside CPM — a cheap CPM with frequency 15 is inefficient.

faqs.question: How does CPM relate to ROI?
faqs.answer: CPM alone doesn't tell you ROI — you need conversion rate and average order value. ROI = (Revenue − Ad Spend) ÷ Ad Spend. Use the ROI calculator to work backwards from your CPM: if 10,000 impressions at £5 CPM gets 50 clicks at 5% conversion and £50 average order, your revenue is £125 on £50 spend — 150% ROI.

faqs.question: What is viewable CPM (vCPM)?
faqs.answer: vCPM charges only for 'viewable' impressions — ads that appear in the visible area of the screen for at least 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video). vCPM is typically higher than standard CPM but ensures you're paying for impressions people can actually see.

faqs.question: Should I optimise for CPM or CPC?
faqs.answer: It depends on your goal. Brand awareness → optimise for CPM and reach. Direct response (traffic, leads, sales) → optimise for CPC and CPA. For many campaigns, the platform's algorithm will auto-optimise toward conversions when you provide enough data.
